items.hook: At least seven people are killed and 32 others are injured after a bomb explodes on an Israeli tour bus at Burgas Airport, Bulgaria.
items.body: The 2012 Burgas bus bombing was a terrorist attack carried out by a suicide bomber on a passenger bus transporting Israeli tourists at the Burgas Airport in Burgas, Bulgaria, on 18 July 2012. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Kit Connor and Joe Locke: 'Making a film like Heartstopper will forever be important'
items.body: The Netflix movie brings Alice Oseman's popular screen adaptation to a conclusion.

items.hook: France and England agree to the Truce of Leulinghem, inaugurating a 13-year peace, the longest period of sustained peace during the Hundred Years' War.
items.body: The Kingdom of France is the historiographical name or umbrella term given to various political entities of France in the medieval and early modern period. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

items.hook: A man sets fire to an anime studio in Fushimi-ku, Kyoto, Japan, killing 36 people and injuring dozens of others.
items.body: The Kyoto Animation arson attack occurred at Kyoto Animation's Studio 1 building in the Fushimi ward of Kyoto, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, on the morning of 18 July 2019. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Third Anglo-Afghan War, also known as the Third Afghan War, the British-Afghan War of 1919, and in Afghanistan as the War of Liberation, was a short war which began on 3 May and ended on 8 August 1919.
items.body: The new Emir of the Emirate of Afghanistan Amanullah Khan declared the holy war of Jihad against the British in the hope to proclaim full independence, as well as to strengthen his own legitimacy. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Anthony Macris is an Australian novelist, critic and academic.
items.body: He has been shortlisted for the Prime Minister's Literary Awards, the Age Book of the Year, and been named a Sydney Morning Herald Best Young Australian Novelist. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Swann Arlaud is a French actor.
items.body: He has appeared in films such as Romantics Anonymous (2010), Elles (2011), Les Anarchistes (2015), The Wakhan Front (2015), Baden Baden (2016), Bloody Milk (2017), By the Grace of God (2019), Valiant Hearts (2021), and Anatomy of a Fall (2023). Worth a quiet t

items.hook: Seth Meyers on Trump 2.0: ‘Every day is a constant stream of humiliation’
items.body:

items.hook: Lauren Laverne diagnosed with blood and bone marrow disorder
items.body: The broadcaster reveals she has smouldering myeloma, which can develop into a type of blood cancer.

items.hook: ‘Maybe the best pumped-up sequel ever made’: James Cameron’s Aliens hits 40
items.body:
The director’s more-is-more approach to the 1986 sequel gave us seat-edge action and an indelible performance from a rule-breaking Sigourney Weaver

items.hook: The Ballot Act 1872 in the United Kingdom introduced the requirement that parliamentary and local government elections be held by secret ballot.
items.body: The Ballot Act 1872 was an act of the Parliament of the United Kingdom that introduced the requirement for parliamentary and local government elections in the United Kingdom to be held by secret ballot. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: A Canadair CL-44 and Sukhoi Su-15 collide in mid-air near Yerevan, Armenia, killing four.
items.body: The Canadair CL-44 was a Canadian turboprop airliner and cargo aircraft based on the Bristol Britannia that was developed and produced by Canadair in the late 1950s and early 1960s. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles

items.hook: The Germans test fly the Messerschmitt Me 262 using its jet engines for the first time.
items.body: The Messerschmitt Me 262, nicknamed Schwalbe in fighter versions, or Sturmvogel in fighter-bomber versions, is a fighter aircraft and fighter-bomber that was designed and produced by the German aircraft manufacturer Messerschmitt. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The All India Kisan Sabha (AIKS), or Akhil Bharatiya Kisan Sabha, is the peasant front of the Communist Party of India (Marxist) and works for farmer's rights, peasant rights, and anti-feudal movement in India.
